'''Had Abbadon''' was an ancient lost mythic [[Planet/Legends|planet]], located in the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]].<ref name="Legacy 28">[[Legacy (2006) 28|''Legacy'' (2006) 28]]</ref> According to some legends, the planet may have been linked to the origin of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] or its forerunners.<ref name="Power of the Jedi">''[[Power of the Jedi Sourcebook]]''</ref>

==History==
During the [[Galactic War]], the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Madreeg]] was searching for the lost world of Had Abbadon.<ref>{{TORcite|exp=shadow|type=class|Sith Inquisitor|Secrets of the Darkest Stars|Rishi}}</ref>

In [[137 ABY]], [[Cade Skywalker]] [[Mission to Had Abbadon|planned to lure]] the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], [[Darth Krayt]], to Had Abbadon in order to [[Assassin/Legends|assassinate]] him and bring down the [[One Sith|Sith Order]] and his [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="Legacy 28" /> By this time, the planet was home to an Imperial garrison loyal to the Sith [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|usurper]].

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Habitation_spheres.jpg|thumb|left|Early concept art for Had Abbadon with two orbiting Death Stars by [[Ralph McQuarrie]]]]

In the [[Star Wars: Episode VI Revenge of the Jedi|Rough Draft]] and Revised Rough Draft of the script for [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']], Had Abbadon was the name of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] capital— which would later be named [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] in the [[Star Wars: The Thrawn Trilogy|Thrawn Trilogy]] and the [[Prequel trilogy|Prequel Trilogy]]. It was a city-planet orbited by two [[Death Star/Legends|Death Stars]] under construction, and a "Green Moon", which the Second Draft of the script would name Jus-Endor and the Revised Second Draft would name [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]. It had an [[Imperial Palace/Legends|Imperial Palace]], the lowest levels of which were home to [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] throne room, surrounded by a lake of lava, around which [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] would duel [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. Starting with the Second Draft of the script, the [[Emperor's throne room]] was moved to the [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star II]].<ref name="annotated">''[[Star Wars: The Annotated Screenplays]]''</ref><ref name="Making of">''[[The Making of Return of the Jedi]]''</ref>

''Abbadon'' is similar to the Hebrew word [[Wikipedia:Abaddon|''abaddon'']], meaning "destruction" or "doom."
